question: Develop a C++ program that reads an integer from the user and determines whether it is positive, negative, or zero using if-else statements.

test_cases.expected: Enter an integer: Invalid input. Please enter an integer.

errors: The if-else structure is functionally correct but uses unnecessary nesting (if (num == 0) else { if (num > 0) else { ... } }) which can reduce readability compared to a direct if-else if-else chain (if (num > 0) else if (num < 0) else ...).
